merge.diff:
diff --git a/src/jinja2/environment.py b/src/jinja2/environment.py
index 9dd455a5..25198b4f 100644
--- a/src/jinja2/environment.py
+++ b/src/jinja2/environment.py
@@ -90,6 +90,22 @@ def create_cache(
     return LRUCache(size)  # type: ignore
 
 
+def _register_loader_environment(
+    loader: t.Optional["BaseLoader"], environment: "Environment"
+) -> None:
+    """Let a loader know that ``environment`` is using it, if the loader
+    supports tracking that (see :class:`~jinja2.loaders.FileSystemLoader`
+    for cache invalidation when watched template files change).
+    """
+    if loader is None:
+        return
+
+    register = getattr(loader, "_register_environment", None)
+
+    if register is not None:
+        register(environment)
+
+
 def copy_cache(
     cache: t.Optional[t.MutableMapping],
 ) -> t.Optional[t.MutableMapping[t.Tuple[weakref.ref, str], "Template"]]:
@@ -355,6 +371,7 @@ class Environment:
         self.cache = create_cache(cache_size)
         self.bytecode_cache = bytecode_cache
         self.auto_reload = auto_reload
+        _register_loader_environment(loader, self)
 
         # configurable policies
         self.policies = DEFAULT_POLICIES.copy()
@@ -426,6 +443,9 @@ class Environment:
             if value is not missing:
                 setattr(rv, key, value)
 
+        if loader is not missing:
+            _register_loader_environment(loader, rv)
+
         if cache_size is not missing:
             rv.cache = create_cache(cache_size)
         else:
diff --git a/src/jinja2/loaders.py b/src/jinja2/loaders.py
index d7d9bd04..0aab9869 100644
--- a/src/jinja2/loaders.py
+++ b/src/jinja2/loaders.py
@@ -170,6 +170,13 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
     :param encoding: Use this encoding to read the text from template
         files.
     :param followlinks: Follow symbolic links in the path.
+    :param auto_reload: Track environments using this loader so their
+        template cache can be invalidated when a template file in one
+        of the search paths is changed, added, or removed. Defaults to
+        ``True``.
+
+    .. versionchanged:: 3.1
+        Added the ``auto_reload`` parameter.
 
     .. versionchanged:: 2.8
         Added the ``followlinks`` parameter.
@@ -180,6 +187,7 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
         searchpath: t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]],
         encoding: str = "utf-8",
         followlinks: bool = False,
+        auto_reload: bool = True,
     ) -> None:
         if not isinstance(searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(searchpath, str):
             searchpath = [searchpath]
@@ -187,11 +195,77 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
         self.searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in searchpath]
         self.encoding = encoding
         self.followlinks = followlinks
+        self.auto_reload = auto_reload
+
+        # Environments using this loader, tracked with weak references
+        # so they can still be garbage collected normally. Only
+        # populated when ``auto_reload`` is enabled.
+        self._environments: "weakref.WeakSet[Environment]" = weakref.WeakSet()
+
+    def _register_environment(self, environment: "Environment") -> None:
+        """Called by :class:`~jinja2.Environment` when it starts using
+        this loader, so that :meth:`_on_template_changed` can find and
+        invalidate its cache entries. Only tracks the environment if
+        ``auto_reload`` is enabled.
+        """
+        if self.auto_reload:
+            self._environments.add(environment)
+
+    def _path_to_template_name(self, filename: str) -> t.Optional[str]:
+        """Convert an absolute file path to the template name that
+        would be used to look it up, or ``None`` if it isn't inside
+        any of this loader's search paths.
+        """
+        filename = os.path.abspath(filename)
+
+        for searchpath in self.searchpath:
+            searchpath = os.path.abspath(searchpath)
+
+            try:
+                common = os.path.commonpath([filename, searchpath])
+            except ValueError:
+                # paths don't have enough in common, e.g. different
+                # drives on Windows
+                continue
+
+            if common != searchpath:
+                continue
+
+            return os.path.relpath(filename, searchpath).replace(os.path.sep, "/")
+
+        return None
+
+    def _on_template_changed(self, file_paths: t.List[str]) -> None:
+        """Invalidate the cache entries for the given file paths in
+        every environment tracked by this loader. ``file_paths`` that
+        don't exist, or aren't inside a search path, are ignored.
+
+        This is called automatically when ``auto_reload`` is enabled
+        and a change is detected in one of the search paths, and can
+        also be called directly, e.g. in tests.
+        """
+        names = {self._path_to_template_name(path) for path in file_paths}
+        names.discard(None)
+
+
